# Afya Medicine Management System - Project Guide

This file provides comprehensive guidance for Claude Code when working with this repository.

## Project Overview

Afya is a comprehensive medicine management system consisting of:
- **back-end**: Spring Boot REST API (Java 17)
- **flutter_mobile**: Flutter mobile application with Clean Architecture
- **Deployment**: Production deployment on OxaHost VPS (102.219.178.221)

## Architecture

### High-Level System Design

The system consists of three main components:
1. Flutter Mobile App (Android/iOS) - Frontend
2. Spring Boot API (Port 8081) - Backend
3. MariaDB/MySQL (Port 3306) - Database

### Communication Flow
- Mobile app connects to API via ngrok tunnel (development) or direct IP (production)
- API endpoint configured in: flutter_mobile/lib/core/constants/api_endpoint.dart
- Current base URL: https://2f6f84353eaf.ngrok-free.app/api
- Production URL: http://102.219.178.221:8081/api

---

## Back-End (Spring Boot)

### Technology Stack
- Framework: Spring Boot 3.4.5
- Language: Java 17
- Build Tool: Maven
- Database: MariaDB (PostgreSQL supported)
- Security: Spring Security with JWT authentication
- Documentation: Swagger/OpenAPI 3
- Email: Spring Mail with Thymeleaf templates
- ORM: Spring Data JPA with Hibernate
- Connection Pool: HikariCP
- Rate Limiting: Bucket4j (100 req/min per IP)

### Key Domain Models
- User: User accounts with authentication
- Group: Collaboration groups (family/healthcare teams)
- GroupMember: Group membership with roles (MANAGER, RESPONSIBLE, MEMBER)
- PharmacyBox: Medicine containers linked to groups
- Medicine: Master medicine database
- MyMedicine: Personal medicine instances in pharmacy boxes
- MedicinePurchaseHistory: Purchase/consumption tracking
- Prescription: Medical prescriptions
- Treatment: Treatment plans
- Reminder: Medication reminders
- Disease: Disease management

### Authentication & Security
- JWT tokens: Access tokens + refresh tokens
- Token expiration: Configurable via properties
- Email verification: Required for account activation
- Password reset: Time-limited reset codes
- Device tracking: Device-specific sessions
- Group-based authorization: Users can only access their group data
- Rate limiting: 100 requests/minute per IP address

### API Endpoints
- /api/auth/** - Authentication (signup, signin, activate, forgot-password)
- /api/groups/** - Group management
- /api/pharmacy-box/** - Pharmacy box operations
- /api/medicines/** - Master medicine database
- /api/my-medicines/** - Personal medicine management
- /api/purchase-history/** - Purchase tracking
- /api/prescriptions/** - Prescription management
- /api/treatments/** - Treatment management
- /api/reminders/** - Reminder management
- /api/group-medical/** - Group medical management for family members
- /api/users/** - User profile management

### Development Commands

Build & Run:
- cd back-end && ./mvnw spring-boot:run - Start application (port 8081)
- ./mvnw clean compile - Clean and compile
- ./mvnw clean package - Build JAR file
- ./mvnw clean package -DskipTests - Build without tests

Code Quality:
- ./mvnw validate - Check formatting (fails on violations)
- ./mvnw spring-javaformat:apply - Auto-format all Java files
- ./format.sh - Bash wrapper for formatting
- ./lint.sh - Bash wrapper for validation

Testing:
- ./mvnw test - Run all tests
- ./mvnw test -Dtest=ClassName - Run specific test class

### Important Notes
- Code formatting: Spring Java Format plugin enforced on build
- CORS: Enabled for all origins (development config)
- Database: Auto-creates/updates schema via spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
- Swagger UI: Available at /swagger-ui/index.html
- Health check: Available at /actuator/health
- File uploads: Stored in uploads/ directory

---

## Flutter Mobile

### Technology Stack
- Framework: Flutter 3.8.0+
- Language: Dart
- State Management: BLoC/Cubit (flutter_bloc)
- Dependency Injection: GetIt
- Routing: go_router (declarative routing)
- HTTP Client: Dio with interceptors
- Local Storage: shared_preferences
- Responsive Design: flutter_screenutil
- Form Validation: formz
- QR Code: qr_flutter, mobile_scanner
- Image Handling: image_picker
- Icons: flutter_svg

### Architecture Pattern: Clean Architecture
The Flutter app follows Clean Architecture with three layers:
- Domain: Entities and repository interfaces (business logic)
- Data: Data sources, models, and repository implementations
- Presentation: BLoC/Cubit, screens, and widgets (UI)

### Key Features
- Authentication Flow: Onboarding → Login → OTP verification → Main app
- Network Monitoring: Real-time connectivity status with AuthBloc
- Token Management: Auto-refresh via AuthInterceptor
- Bottom Navigation: Persistent state with StatefulShellRoute
- Profile Management: User profile with image upload
- Group Collaboration: Create/join groups, manage members
- Group Medical Management: Manage family members medical data

### Development Commands

Flutter Commands:
- cd flutter_mobile && flutter pub get - Get dependencies
- flutter run - Run app (debug mode)
- flutter run --release - Run in release mode
- flutter build apk - Build Android APK
- flutter build ios - Build iOS app
- flutter clean - Clean build files
- flutter analyze - Analyze code

### Important Notes
- API Connection: Currently using ngrok tunnel for development
- Responsive Design: Uses flutter_screenutil with base size 411.4x914.3
- Network Status: Monitored by AuthBloc, shows snackbar alerts
- Local Storage: Token, refresh token, onboarding status in SharedPreferences
- Font: Custom Roboto font (Regular, Medium, Bold)

---

## Development Workflow

### Starting the Application

1. Start Back-End:
   cd back-end && ./mvnw spring-boot:run
   Application runs on http://localhost:8081
   Swagger UI: http://localhost:8081/swagger-ui/index.html

2. Start Flutter App:
   cd flutter_mobile && flutter pub get && flutter run

### Making Changes

Back-End Changes:
1. Modify Java code in src/main/java/com/idvey/afya/
2. Run ./format.sh to auto-format code
3. Run ./lint.sh to check for violations
4. Test changes with ./mvnw test
5. Restart app with ./mvnw spring-boot:run

Flutter Changes:
1. Modify Dart code in lib/
2. Hot reload: Press r in terminal (fast)
3. Hot restart: Press R in terminal (full restart)
4. Run flutter analyze to check for issues

### Environment Variables

Back-End:
- SECRET_KEY: JWT signing secret (required)
- Database credentials can be overridden via environment variables

Flutter:
- Update API endpoint in lib/core/constants/api_endpoint.dart
- For production: Change to http://102.219.178.221:8081/api
- For development: Use ngrok tunnel URL

---

## Deployment

### Production Environment
- VPS Provider: OxaHost
- Server: srv203017
- OS: Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS
- Public IP: 102.219.178.221
- Application Port: 8081 (8085 in production config)
- Web Access: Port 80 via Nginx reverse proxy

See complete_deployment_guide.md for full deployment instructions.

---

## Common Tasks

### Adding a New API Endpoint
1. Create/update entity in models/
2. Create/update repository in repository/
3. Create/update service in security/service/
4. Create/update controller in controllers/
5. Add request/response DTOs in payload/
6. Run ./format.sh before committing
7. Update Swagger annotations for documentation

### Adding a New Flutter Screen
1. Create entity in domain/entities/
2. Create model in data/model/
3. Create repository interface in domain/repositories/
4. Implement repository in data/repositories/
5. Create data source in data/data_sources/
6. Create cubit in presentation/bloc/
7. Create screen in presentation/screens/
8. Register dependencies in injection_container.dart
9. Add route in config/router/app_route.dart

### Updating API Endpoint in Flutter
1. Edit flutter_mobile/lib/core/constants/api_endpoint.dart
2. Update baseurl constant
3. Hot restart the app

---

## Security Considerations

### Back-End
- All endpoints except /api/auth/**, /swagger-ui/**, and health checks require JWT
- Passwords hashed with BCrypt
- Group-based authorization enforces data isolation
- Rate limiting prevents abuse
- CORS configured for development (restrict in production)

### Flutter
- Token stored in SharedPreferences (consider secure storage for production)
- Auto-refresh tokens via interceptor
- Network status monitoring
- Device info tracking for sessions

### Production
- Change JWT secret to strong, unique value
- Update email credentials
- Configure SSL/TLS (Let''s Encrypt)
- Set up fail2ban for SSH protection
- Regular system updates

---
